experiment: F-HS1 temporal dynamics: where compression fails across granularity levels
prior_work: S375 established three-tier compaction gradient (tools 55% > principles 12.3% > lessons 2.7%). This experiment adds temporal dynamics and identifies the granularity mechanism.
method: Analyze lesson survival rates, proxy-K sawtooth dynamics, production-compaction ratios across 5 eras (S1-S422), and granularity-level compaction mechanisms. N=856 lesson files, 63 proxy-K measurements, 670 session-attributed lessons.
expect: Two regimes (early rapid, late stabilization). Half-life ~150 sessions. Compaction is sunset mechanism.

principle_lifecycle.note: Principles DO get retired (22% gap), unlike lessons (0% deletion)

findings.granularity_gap: Compression failure operates at the wrong granularity level. Swarm has CONTENT-level compaction (20-line trim, SUPERSEDED pointers) but ZERO UNIT-level compaction (lesson deletion). 856 lessons, 0 deletions, 100% survival across all eras. Compaction events (298 commits) modify content within units but never remove units.
findings.three_regimes_not_two: Expected 2 regimes but found 3: growth (S1-S200, 0.6-1.1 L/s), dormancy (S201-S300, 0.09 L/s), explosion (S301+, 4.0 L/s). No stabilization regime exists — production ACCELERATED 40x from dormancy. Expect FALSIFIED.
findings.sawtooth_dynamics: Proxy-K follows a reactive sawtooth cycle: 23-session mean period, 4.5% mean amplitude. Growth → threshold → compaction → temporary drop → resumed growth. Long-term trajectory monotonically increasing despite 15 compaction events. Reactive compaction is bounded — it creates oscillations but cannot reverse the trend.
findings.layer_asymmetry: Three compaction granularities coexist: lessons (content-only, 0% unit removal), principles (22% unit retirement via subsumption), tools (55% unit archival). The binding constraint is the LOWEST compaction level — lesson accumulation drives proxy-K because it's the largest store with zero unit-level compaction.
findings.bureaucratic_isomorphism: Exact structural match: regulations get amended (content-level) but rarely repealed (unit-level). The US CFR grew 20K→185K pages because amendment exists but repeal doesn't. Same mechanism: content compaction saturates when units reach minimum size; the only remaining lever is unit deletion, which no mechanism supports.

compaction_is_sunset.actual: compact.py is content-trim, not sunset. No sunset mechanism exists for lessons

actual: L4 finding: compression failure operates at the wrong granularity level. Content-level compaction (trim, amend) exists but unit-level compaction (delete, repeal) is absent. 100% lesson survival (N=856), proxy-K sawtooth (23s period, 4.5% amplitude, monotonically increasing). Three production regimes not two. Bureaucratic isomorphism exact: amendment without repeal = structural sclerosis.
diff: All 3 expectations falsified or partially falsified. Did NOT predict infinite half-life, 3 regimes, or the granularity gap (main finding). Expected stabilization, got acceleration. The finding is more specific than the expectation: it identifies WHERE compression fails (unit vs content level), not just THAT it fails.
